headline: JPMorgan has a stark message on climate change
summary: Businesses and investors are always thinking about risk. Risk from things that haven’t happened yet but could happen in the future. That could be anything from the risk of getting sued to an economic crisis or a risk from a cybersecurity threat. Many companies try to plan for these types of risk ...

headline: How The Tesco (LSE:TSCO) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Targets And Buybacks
summary: Tesco’s fair value estimate in recent analyst models now sits at £5.10, compared with £4.89 previously, putting a fresh spotlight on how price targets are being recalibrated. Recent updates from large banks, including a 20 GBp move at JPMorgan and a 10 GBp change at Deutsche Bank, show how both confidence and caution are being reflected in these revised targets. Read on to see what is driving this evolving narrative and how you can keep track of the shifting assumptions behind it. Analyst...

headline: A $900,000 Stock Portfolio Exposed to Just Three Sectors Is a Retirement Time Bomb
summary: At 63, with $900,000 in a brokerage account split across tech (45%), healthcare (30%), and financials (25%), you have built something real. The five holdings — Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ), UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH), and JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) — are legitimate blue-chip businesses. The problem is what happens to all three sectors ... A $900,000 Stock Portfolio Exposed to Just Three Sectors Is a Retirement Time Bomb

headline: JPMorgan Announces Cash Distributions for the JPMorgan ETFs
summary: TORONTO, April 24, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- J. P. Morgan Asset Management (JPMAM)* today announced the final April 2026 cash distributions for the below listed JPMorgan ETFs. The JPMorgan ETFs trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX). Unitholders of record on May 1, 2026 will receive cash distributions payable on May 7, 2026. Details of the “per unit” distributions are as follows: JPMorgan ETF nameTicker symbolDistribution per unit ($)Payment frequencyJPMorgan US Equity Premium Income Active ET

headline: American Express Gets Dueling Calls From BofA and Barclays: Which Side Is Right?
summary: American Express‘s (NYSE:AXP) Q1 2026 earnings beat drew a split verdict from Wall Street. Bank of America reiterated its Buy rating and raised its price target to $387 from $381, while Barclays kept an Equal Weight rating and trimmed its price target to $322 from $323. The disagreement cuts to the heart of the premium ... American Express Gets Dueling Calls From BofA and Barclays: Which Side Is Right?

headline: JPMorgan Initiates Hims & Hers With Overweight: Could the Novo Nordisk Deal Be the Turning Point?
summary: Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) just picked up a high-profile endorsement. The call lands with HIMS stock trading at $30.46. For long-term investors, ... JPMorgan Initiates Hims & Hers With Overweight: Could the Novo Nordisk Deal Be the Turning Point?

headline: How The F5 (FFIV) Story Is Shifting With AI Security Trends And Valuation Tweaks
summary: F5’s analyst fair value estimate has shifted slightly, with the price target moving to $311.30 from $310.30, a modest change but one that still matters if you are tracking entry points and expectations. Recent research tying resilient cybersecurity demand and the role of AI coding assistants to higher targets from firms like JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs helps explain why some analysts are willing to assign a bit more room in their valuation work. As you read on, you will see how to interpret...
